task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image is opened in ImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ application window displaying the 'blobs.gif' image, which consists of dark irregular blob shapes on a light background.

task_completion.description: Verify that the watershed segmentation algorithm has been applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The blobs image in ImageJ appears binary (black/white) and shows watershed lines (typically 1-pixel wide lines separating touching blobs).

task_completion.description: Verify that the individual measurements of the separated objects are saved to the specified CSV path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or editor showing 'watershed_measurements.csv' located in the '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory, containing tabular measurement data (like Area, Mean, etc.) for the objects.

task_completion.description: Verify that a summary text file is created at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing a file named 'watershed_summary.txt' located in the '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the contents of the summary text file document the correct object counts and their difference.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor displaying the contents of 'watershed_summary.txt' showing the object count before watershed (64), the object count after watershed (131), and the difference representing new objects resolved (67).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the ImageJ GUI to perform the image analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: ImageJ application windows, toolbars, and menus (like Process > Binary > Watershed or Analyze > Analyze Particles) are visible in the screenshots, rather than just terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually measured the objects using ImageJ.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ImageJ 'Results' or 'Summary' table window is visible on screen displaying the actual counts (64 before, 131 after) before they are written to the text file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just manually type the expected output files without processing the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of intermediate image processing steps on screen (e.g., thresholding, making binary, applying watershed) proving the image was actually manipulated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Blobs (25K)' sample image is 'blobs.gif'. When properly thresholded and analyzed in ImageJ, the object count before watershed is 64, and the object count after watershed is 131 (yielding a difference of 67). The maximum area of an object is 902 pixels. The measurements must be saved to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/watershed_measurements.csv' and the summary to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/watershed_summary.txt'.
